📖 Methodology & Indicator Guide

RSI (14) — Relative Strength Index measures momentum on a 0-100 scale. Below 30 = oversold (potential buy), above 70 = overbought (potential sell).

MACD (12,26,9) — Moving Average Convergence Divergence. Histogram rising = bullish momentum, falling = bearish momentum.

SMA Trend — Price position relative to 50-day and 200-day Simple Moving Averages. Price above both = uptrend. Golden Cross (SMA50 crosses above SMA200) = bullish signal.

BB %B — Bollinger Band %B shows where price sits relative to the 20-day bands (0 = lower band, 1 = upper band). Below 0 = oversold, above 1 = overbought.

Volatility — Annualised standard deviation of daily returns (trailing 1 year).

Sharpe Ratio — Risk-adjusted return: (annualised return - 4.35% RBA rate) / volatility. Above 1.0 = good, above 2.0 = excellent.

Max Drawdown — Largest peak-to-trough decline in the trailing 1-year period.

Quant Score — Weighted composite: SMA trend (30%), RSI (20%), MACD (20%), Sharpe (20%), Bollinger (10%). Scale: -10 (very bearish) to +10 (very bullish).
